skills.aiInstallPrompt:
Install the skill 'gpeople-lookup' by writing the file below.

Universal path: ./skills/gpeople-lookup/SKILL.md
Grok path: ~/.grok/skills/gpeople-lookup/SKILL.md
Cursor path: ~/.cursor/skills/gpeople-lookup/SKILL.md
Claude path: ~/.claude/skills/gpeople-lookup/SKILL.md
VS Code agent path: .github/skills/gpeople-lookup/SKILL.md

Create the folder if needed, then write SKILL.md with this exact content:

```markdown
---
name: gpeople-lookup
description: >
  Look up contact before drafting communication. Requires integrations: @google-contacts. Uses search_tool + use_tool. Cadence: on-demand. Triggers: "gpeople lookup", /gpeople-lookup.
metadata:
  category: google-people
  type: connector
  cadence: on-demand
  risk: low
  connectors: google-people
  integrations: @google-contacts
allowed-tools: search_tool, use_tool
---
# /gpeople-lookup

Look up contact before drafting communication.

## Works with

Use this as a portable `SKILL.md` in Grok, Claude, Cursor, ChatGPT, Codex, VS Code agent skills, Gemini, or any AI workspace that supports reusable markdown instructions. If a tool does not support slash commands, paste the task and ask the AI to follow this skill.

## When to use

The user is about to email, call, or meet someone and needs their details fast - correct spelling of the name, current title and company, which email address to use, and any notes from past interactions. Run this before drafting outreach so the communication uses the right facts.

## Inputs to gather

Check context first; only ask if missing:

- Who to look up (name, partial name, company, or email fragment - whatever the user has)
- What the lookup is for (drafting an email, meeting prep, phone call) - this decides which fields matter
- If multiple matches are likely (common name), any disambiguating detail (company, city, when they last interacted)

## Tone & voice

- **Tone:** Respect contact privacy. Confirm before mass lookups.
- **Audience:** Infer from user context.

## Connector access (required)

This skill **cannot run from text alone** - it must call live integrations.
Before any workflow step:

1. Check your AI tool's connector settings and confirm each integration below is enabled and authenticated (Grok: run `/mcps` · Claude: Settings → Connectors · Cursor: Settings → MCP · ChatGPT: enable the app in settings · other tools: ask your AI what app connections it has).
2. Use `search_tool` to discover tools for each connector (search by app name).
3. Call integrations with `use_tool` and fully-qualified names (`server__tool_name`).
4. Connector tool names vary by AI tool. Discover the available tools first, then call the exact tool name exposed in the current session.

### Required integrations

- **@google-contacts** - Google Contacts
  - MCP server key: `google-people`
  - Example tools: `google-people__search_contacts`, `google-people__get_contact`

### If a connector is missing

Stop immediately. Tell the user:

```
This skill needs <@mention> enabled.
1. Open your AI tool's connector/integration settings (Grok: `/mcps` · Claude: Settings → Connectors · Cursor: Settings → MCP · ChatGPT: app settings)
2. Enable and authenticate <app>
3. Refresh your tool list, then run this skill again
```

Do **not** pretend you accessed Gmail/Canva/Drive without a successful `use_tool` call.

## Workflow

1. Search Google Contacts with the identifier given. If nothing matches exactly, retry with looser variants (surname only, company name) before reporting no match.
2. If multiple contacts match, show a short disambiguation list (name, company, email domain) and ask the user to pick - never guess between two different people.
3. Pull the chosen contact's details: full name (as spelled in the contact record), title/organization, all email addresses and phone numbers on file, and any notes or labels attached to the contact.
4. Highlight staleness signals honestly: if the record has no organization, an old-looking title, or conflicting emails, say so - the user is about to rely on this for real communication.
5. Recommend which email/phone to use when several exist (work vs personal), based on the stated purpose, and say why.
6. This skill is read-only - do not create, edit, or merge contacts. If the user asks to update the record, propose the exact change and wait for explicit approval.

## Output format

```
Contact: <Full Name>  (matched on: "<search term>")
Title/Org: <title>, <organization>   [or "not on file"]
Recommended for <purpose>: <email/phone>  - <one-line why>

All on file:
| Field | Value | Note |
|---|---|---|
| Email (work) | ... | recommended |
| Email (other) | ... | |
| Phone | ... | |
| Labels/Notes | ... | |

Freshness: <anything that looks stale or conflicting, or "record looks current">
```

## Do

- Show the disambiguation list whenever more than one contact matches - never silently pick one
- Use the name spelling exactly as stored in the contact record when drafting follow-on communication
- Flag stale or conflicting fields plainly before the user relies on them
- Ask at most ONE clarifying question if critical info is missing

## Don't

- Don't guess between two people with similar names - a mis-addressed email is the exact failure this skill exists to prevent
- Don't fabricate a title or company when the record lacks one - say "not on file"
- Don't modify, merge, or delete contact records - this skill is read-only
- Don't dump every contact matching a broad term - cap the disambiguation list and ask for a narrower identifier

## Quality check (before responding)

- [ ] Output matches requested tone and channel
- [ ] No placeholder brackets left unfilled (use sensible defaults or ask)
- [ ] User can copy-paste the result immediately
- [ ] Required integrations (@google-contacts) verified in your AI tool's connector settings before `use_tool` calls
- [ ] Connector actions proposed, not auto-applied (unless read-only)
```

After writing the file, confirm it exists. The user can then run /gpeople-lookup or describe the task from the skill description.

skills.aiInstallPrompt:
Install the skill 'gpeople-org-chart' by writing the file below.

Universal path: ./skills/gpeople-org-chart/SKILL.md
Grok path: ~/.grok/skills/gpeople-org-chart/SKILL.md
Cursor path: ~/.cursor/skills/gpeople-org-chart/SKILL.md
Claude path: ~/.claude/skills/gpeople-org-chart/SKILL.md
VS Code agent path: .github/skills/gpeople-org-chart/SKILL.md

Create the folder if needed, then write SKILL.md with this exact content:

```markdown
---
name: gpeople-org-chart
description: >
  Map org relationships from directory. Requires integrations: @google-contacts. Uses search_tool + use_tool. Cadence: on-demand. Triggers: "gpeople org chart", /gpeople-org-chart.
metadata:
  category: google-people
  type: connector
  cadence: on-demand
  risk: low
  connectors: google-people
  integrations: @google-contacts
allowed-tools: search_tool, use_tool
---
# /gpeople-org-chart

Map org relationships from directory.

## Works with

Use this as a portable `SKILL.md` in Grok, Claude, Cursor, ChatGPT, Codex, VS Code agent skills, Gemini, or any AI workspace that supports reusable markdown instructions. If a tool does not support slash commands, paste the task and ask the AI to follow this skill.

## When to use

The user is preparing for a sales deal, a partnership, or a new-job onboarding and needs to understand who's who at a target organization: reporting lines, roles, and who the likely decision-maker vs. influencer is - built from what their own contact directory actually knows.

## Inputs to gather

Check context first; only ask if missing:

- Which organization (company/domain) to map
- The purpose (sales stakeholder map, onboarding, partner intro) - this decides whether to emphasize decision authority or team structure
- Any people the user already knows there (anchors the map and fills gaps the directory can't)

## Tone & voice

- **Tone:** Respect contact privacy. Confirm before mass lookups.
- **Audience:** Infer from user context.

## Connector access (required)

This skill **cannot run from text alone** - it must call live integrations.
Before any workflow step:

1. Check your AI tool's connector settings and confirm each integration below is enabled and authenticated (Grok: run `/mcps` · Claude: Settings → Connectors · Cursor: Settings → MCP · ChatGPT: enable the app in settings · other tools: ask your AI what app connections it has).
2. Use `search_tool` to discover tools for each connector (search by app name).
3. Call integrations with `use_tool` and fully-qualified names (`server__tool_name`).
4. Connector tool names vary by AI tool. Discover the available tools first, then call the exact tool name exposed in the current session.

### Required integrations

- **@google-contacts** - Google Contacts
  - MCP server key: `google-people`
  - Example tools: `google-people__search_contacts`, `google-people__get_contact`

### If a connector is missing

Stop immediately. Tell the user:

```
This skill needs <@mention> enabled.
1. Open your AI tool's connector/integration settings (Grok: `/mcps` · Claude: Settings → Connectors · Cursor: Settings → MCP · ChatGPT: app settings)
2. Enable and authenticate <app>
3. Refresh your tool list, then run this skill again
```

Do **not** pretend you accessed Gmail/Canva/Drive without a successful `use_tool` call.

## Workflow

1. Pull every contact whose email domain or organization field matches the target company. State the count found - a map built from 4 contacts is a sketch, not an org chart, and the user should know which.
2. Extract role signals from titles: seniority band (C-level / VP / Director / Manager / IC) and function (engineering, product, sales, finance, etc.). Normalize obvious variants ("Eng Lead" ≈ "Engineering Manager").
3. Infer structure only where evidence supports it: explicit titles ("VP of X" likely above "Director of X"), notes fields mentioning reporting lines, and the user's own knowledge from step-0 inputs. Mark every inferred line as *inferred* - a contact directory is not an HR system.
4. For sales/partnership purposes, tag each mapped person with a likely stakeholder role: economic buyer, technical evaluator, champion, or gatekeeper - clearly labeled as hypothesis to validate, not fact.
5. Name the gaps explicitly: functions with zero contacts, seniority layers with nobody in them, and stale records (old titles). Suggest who the user should try to get introduced to, based on what's missing for their purpose.
6. This skill is read-only against the directory; it never edits contacts and never contacts anyone.

## Output format

```
Org map: <Company>  (built from <N> contacts in your directory - <coverage: sparse/partial/good>)

<Seniority-grouped tree, inferred lines marked>
├─ <Name> - <Title> (C-level)
│   ├─ <Name> - <Title> (*reports-to inferred from titles*)
│   └─ ...

Stakeholder read (for <purpose>) - hypotheses to validate:
| Person | Function | Likely role | Why |
|---|---|---|---|

Gaps and next intros:
- No contacts in <function> - consider asking <known person> for an intro
- <Name>'s title may be stale (last note dated <year>)
```

## Do

- State how many directory contacts the map is built from, up front
- Mark every inferred reporting line as inferred - never present directory guesses as org fact
- Tailor the stakeholder tags to the stated purpose
- Ask at most ONE clarifying question if critical info is missing

## Don't

- Don't present an org chart built from a handful of contacts as authoritative - call it a sketch
- Don't fabricate names, titles, or reporting lines the directory doesn't contain
- Don't include personal contact details (personal phones/emails) in a map that may be shared with a team
- Don't email or contact anyone - this skill only maps

## Quality check (before responding)

- [ ] Output matches requested tone and channel
- [ ] No placeholder brackets left unfilled (use sensible defaults or ask)
- [ ] User can copy-paste the result immediately
- [ ] Required integrations (@google-contacts) verified in your AI tool's connector settings before `use_tool` calls
- [ ] Connector actions proposed, not auto-applied (unless read-only)
```

After writing the file, confirm it exists. The user can then run /gpeople-org-chart or describe the task from the skill description.
